summary: FairXI v1.1 fully validated. Backend: 11/11 pytest PASS covering P0 rating engine (win/loss delta signs, draw actual=0.5, clamp [1,5], MVP votes counted, rating-history endpoint returns current_rating/matches_played/history[old_rating,new_rating,delta,opp_avg]), snake_draft uses effective_rating (star player with dynamic~5 kept high rating despite seed=1 in second match — current_rating stayed >=2, not collapsed), backward-compat (mark-played with no body works; GET /matches/{id} still 200 for ungrouped), P1 groups (POST /groups returns 32+ char admin_token, GET dashboard 403 on wrong token, aggregated standings sorted by wins*3+draws, mvp_leaderboard, top_gainers all populated after a played group match; POST /groups/{id}/admin/{token}/matches sets group_id on match doc — verified via GET /matches/{id}), plus regression smoke (demo endpoint, admin_token length >=30, public match view hides admin_token). Frontend: /groups Create Group form renders, submit creates group and navigates to /group/:id/:token dashboard. Dashboard renders standings-panel, mvp-leaderboard-panel, top-gainers-panel, season-recap-panel, group-name, new-group-match. download-season-recap click completes with ZERO JS errors. new-group-match navigates to /group/:id/:token/new-match; form submit creates group match and navigates to /created/:id/:token. Admin panel with status=teams_generated shows winner-picker with mark-winner-1, mark-winner-2, mark-draw; clicking mark-winner-1 advances match to played (winner-picker disappears, toast 'Marked as played · 2 players climbed'). broadcast-panel visible; broadcast-whatsapp-btn calls window.open with https://wa.me/?text=... URL (verified via intercept). PaymentDeepLink: enable-pay-link toggles pay-link-panel; picking paypalme provider + handle 'testhandle' yields pay-open-btn href https://www.paypal.com/paypalme/testhandle/20.00 (matches spec); switching to revolut yields https://revolut.me/testhandle?amount=20.00 (matches spec).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~900 lines — approaching the 700-line threshold. Consider splitting into routers (matches.py, groups.py, mvp.py, ratings.py) and a services layer before next release.

GroupDashboard aggregation is O(N*M) — for each match it scans players_across/votes_across in Python. Fine for small groups (<50 matches, <500 players) but should be pushed to Mongo aggregation pipeline if user counts grow.

recalculate_after_match reads mvp_votes once per match — good. But mark-played is now non-idempotent: calling it twice will double-increment matches_played on player_stats and add duplicate rating_history rows. Consider guarding: if match.status already == 'played', return existing rating_changes (recompute from db.rating_history for that match_id) or reject with 409.

PAY_PROVIDERS builders don't validate amount format — a non-numeric share would produce 'amount=NaN' in the URL. Callsite in AdminPanel does Number(share||0).toFixed(2) so it's currently safe, but the lib fn should defend itself too.

Rating engine K=0.15 is very small — a lone win against equal opposition only shifts rating by ~0.075. Users may perceive the dynamic rating as static. Consider surfacing +/- deltas per match in PlayerHistory (which the sparkline already does).

success_rate.frontend: 100% for all P0/P1/P2/P3 items in the v1.1 review

seed_data_creation: Ephemeral test matches/groups created via API with TEST_ prefix. No cleanup endpoint; rate-limit prevents flooding.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The snake_draft dynamic-rating test cannot fully disambiguate ordering with only 4 players (any 4-player 2-team snake produces the same team split regardless of order). Instead, validation was done by checking that after 2 matches, the star player's current_rating in rating-history did NOT collapse to their new low seed — proving the engine used dynamic. For deeper coverage, do an 8-player draft with 3 dynamic-heavy phones and assert their team placements match a snake ordered by dynamic values.
